2604.18452.title: ESsEN: Training Compact Discriminative Vision-Language Transformers in a Low-Resource Setting
2604.18452.paper_summary: Vision-language modeling is rapidly increasing in popularity with an ever expanding list of available models. In most cases, these vision-language models have parameters in the tens of billions, which is necessary for some needs, but in many cases smaller models are necessary (e.g., on edge devices or independent robotic platforms). Unfortunately, there is little research in producing light-weight models or in training them with small datasets. Inspired by the language learning progression and data sparsity in child development, in this paper, we address both of these goals in a systematic fashion. We show that two-tower encoder models are superior to one-tower encoders in low-resource settings for discriminative English tasks. We show also that incorporating traditional convolutional networks into the two-tower transformer architecture can help produce parameter efficient vision-language models. Finally, we show that the cross-modal fusion module of two-tower encoders can vary significantly in shape and size while producing the same results. In addition, we present ESsEN, a compact vision-language model that can be trained end-to-end with relatively few resources that performs as well on several tasks with only a fraction of the parameters compared to other models. The experimental results and the tools we present here make vision-language modeling more accessible to a wider variety of researchers.
2604.18452.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言建模的受歡迎程度正在迅速增加，並且可用模型的清單不斷擴展。<lb/>在大多數情況下，這些視覺-語言模型的參數達到數十億，這對於某些需求是必要的，但在許多情況下，需要較小的模型（例如，在邊緣設備或獨立的機器人平台上）。<lb/>不幸的是，針對輕量級模型的研究或使用小數據集進行訓練的研究相對較少。<lb/>受到語言學習進程和兒童發展中數據稀疏性的啟發，在本文中，我們以系統的方式解決這兩個目標。<lb/>我們展示了在低資源環境中，雙塔編碼器模型在區分英語任務方面優於單塔編碼器。<lb/>我們還展示了將傳統卷積網絡納入雙塔Transformer架構可以幫助產生參數高效的視覺-語言模型。<lb/>最後，我們展示了雙塔編碼器的跨模態融合模塊在形狀和大小上可以有顯著的變化，同時產生相同的結果。<lb/>此外，我們提出了ESsEN，一種緊湊的視覺-語言模型，可以用相對較少的資源進行端到端訓練，並且在多個任務上表現良好，參數僅為其他模型的一小部分。<lb/>我們在此展示的實驗結果和工具使得視覺-語言建模對更廣泛的研究人員變得更加可及。
2604.18452.author: Clayton Fields et.al.
